posts.name: Barry Rothof
posts.cooked:
• 2016 Jean-Claude Ramonet Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Clos du Cailleret - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru (10/28/2018)

Busting from the glass with a complex bouquet of apple, peach and hazelnut. On the palate the wine is concentrated, layered and deep underpinned by bright acidity and citrus with real cut and tension through the finish. Fantastic stuff! (93 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: not to be confused w Cailleret. I posted on the 2014, their first year w this vineyard. Not sure why it’s considered a monopole when Chartron makes it in 2016 also. Bought some yesterday, as a matter of fact. Girardin made a less expensive version, haven’t tasted it. Would love to learn more.

posts.name: Alex Goldstein
posts.cooked:
Chartron makes Clos Du Cailleret from Puligny, this Ramonet is from Chassagne.

not to be confused w Cailleret. I posted on the 2014, their first year w this vineyard. Not sure why it’s considered a monopole when Chartron makes it in 2016 also. Bought some yesterday, as a matter of fact. Girardin made a less expensive version, haven’t tasted it. Would love to learn more.
